title: Refutation
description: The refuter attacks a design BEFORE code exists. Its job is to be wrong loudly: a surviving design is stronger for it, a dying design just saved an implementation cycle. The schema forces argument either way — assent without argument does not typecheck.

argument.description: The concrete failure: inputs/state → wrong result. Not vibes.

evidence.description: path:line or observed behavior backing the attack.

outcome.description: lands = design must change; deflected = design already handles it (say how); withdrawn = attack was wrong (say why).

attacks.description: Every attack attempted, INCLUDING the ones that failed — deflected attacks are the evidence the design was actually tested.

gates_assert_outcomes.description: Do ALL acceptance gates assert forward progress/outcomes rather than history shape? (rigid step 3)

holes.description: Ways the implementation could pass every gate and still be wrong. Each hole becomes a gate revision.
